_notice: ⚠️ LEGACY REFERENCE ONLY - This file is kept for historical reference and detailed configuration documentation. It is NOT used by GitHub Copilot CLI v1.0+. For active configuration, see .github/copilot/settings.json
_legacy_reason: This file was the project-specific orchestration configuration before migration to GitHub Copilot CLI v1.0+ standard structure. It contains valuable context about how the multi-agent system was designed and evolved.
_documentation_location: docs/copilot/ - See ENHANCEMENT-SUMMARY.md and RESEARCH-ENHANCEMENTS.md for context on how this configuration evolved
system: GitHub Copilot CLI Co-work Session Configuration

enforcement.description: All operations route through approved agents defined in rules.json

execution_planning.description: Execution planning documents guide orchestrator and agent behavior
optional_sections_for_feedback_tracking: PR Review Feedback Cycles: Track feedback per PR across phases 3B-3F
Feature Branch Registry: Map PR numbers to feature branches
Feedback Resolution Log: Document feedback addressed per cycle

feedback_tracking_format.description: Structure for tracking PR feedback cycles in execution plan
feedback_tracking_format.cycle_tracking: #### Cycle 1: Initial Review | Cycle 2: Feedback Fix | Cycle 3: Re-Review
feedback_tracking_format.registry_format: | PR # | Feature Branch | Issue | Cycle Status | Next Action |

multi_agent_orchestration.description: Orchestrator agent delegates to developer agents based on execution plan

workflow.phase_1_planning: Orchestrator analyzes execution plan and GitHub issues
workflow.phase_2_delegation: Orchestrator creates branches and delegates to agents
workflow.phase_3a_initial_implementation: Developer agents implement features per execution plan on feature branches
workflow.phase_3b_reviewer_examines: Reviewer examines PR, documents findings, provides feedback
workflow.phase_3c_developer_feedback_cycle: Developer tracks EXISTING feature branch from PR, implements fixes (steps 11-23 in rules.json)
workflow.phase_3d_pr_auto_update: Developer pushes fixes to same branch; PR auto-updates with new commits
workflow.phase_3e_reviewer_re_reviews: Reviewer re-examines same PR; loop to 3C if more fixes needed, or approve
workflow.phase_3f_approval_consolidation_ready: PR approved; ready to move to Phase 4 consolidation
workflow.phase_4_consolidation: Reviewer agent creates work/phase-<N>-consolidation, merges all approved feature branches, runs tests
workflow.phase_5_merge: GitHub Actions merges approved PRs using gh CLI --merge

feedback_cycle_handling.description: Explicit handling of PR review feedback in existing feature branches
feedback_cycle_handling.phases_3b_to_3f: Sub-phases within implementation phase for feedback handling
feedback_cycle_handling.critical_principle: Reuse EXISTING feature branch linked to PR; NO new branch creation
feedback_cycle_handling.key_command: gh pr view <PR-NUMBER> --json headRefName (identifies feature branch)

agent_git_workflow.description: Delegated agents follow strict git workflow before any operation

step_4_file_tracking.method: git status, git diff per commit
step_4_file_tracking.feature_branch_requirement: All changes on feature branch only

examples: fix(#40): Fix keyboard navigation in modal
refactor(#32): Optimize N+1 query with DataLoader

pr_feedback_cycle_configuration.description: Configuration for handling PR review feedback while reusing existing feature branches

phase_3b_reviewer_examines.description: Reviewer examines PR and documents findings

phase_3c_developer_feedback_handling.description: Developer implements fixes on EXISTING feature branch (steps 11-23)
phase_3c_developer_feedback_handling.critical_rule: REUSE feature branch from PR; DO NOT create new branch

workflow_steps.step_12: Identify feature branch: gh pr view <PR-NUMBER> --json headRefName
workflow_steps.step_14: Switch to EXISTING: git switch feat/issue-#<N>-... (🔴 CRITICAL)
workflow_steps.step_19: Commit: git commit -m 'fix(#<N>): Address review feedback'
workflow_steps.step_20: Push to SAME branch: git push origin feat/issue-#<N>-...
workflow_steps.step_22: Update execution plan with Cycle progress

key_commands: gh pr view <PR> --json headRefName (get exact branch)
git push origin feat/issue-#<N>-... (push to same branch)

phase_3d_pr_auto_update.description: PR automatically updates with new commits
phase_3d_pr_auto_update.notification: Reviewer is notified of new commits
phase_3d_pr_auto_update.no_new_pr: Same PR #, no duplicate PR created

phase_3e_reviewer_re_reviews.description: Reviewer examines new commits on same PR
actions: Outcome: Approve or request more fixes
phase_3e_reviewer_re_reviews.loop_condition: If more fixes needed, loop back to phase_3c

branch_registry_pattern.description: Track PR numbers and feature branches in execution plan
branch_registry_pattern.format: | PR # | Feature Branch | Issue | Cycle Status | Next Action |
branch_registry_pattern.purpose: Quick lookup for orchestrator and developer

consolidation_after_feedback.description: Only start Phase 4 consolidation after all PRs complete feedback cycles
consolidation_after_feedback.precondition: All open PRs must have Phase 3F status (Approved)
consolidation_after_feedback.cleanup: Delete consolidation branch after testing (no PR created from work branch)

gh_cli_integration.description: Use GitHub gh CLI for all GitHub operations

pr_feedback_operations.description: GitHub CLI operations for handling PR feedback cycles

identify_feature_branch.purpose: Get exact feature branch from PR (Phase 3C Step 12)
identify_feature_branch.critical: Used to identify which branch to switch to for fixes

view_pr_details.purpose: Get full PR context including feedback comments

close_pr.use_case: Rarely used; PRs auto-close on merge

merge_strategy.description: Use --merge flag to keep commit history
merge_strategy.single_branch_benefit: Clean merge of single feature branch per issue (no multiple branches)

github_actions_automation.description: GitHub Actions automates PR merge to main
github_actions_automation.trigger: When PR is reviewed and approved

workflow.step_1: Developer opens PR from feature branch

pr_consolidation.description: Reviewer creates consolidation branch to review multiple PRs together
pr_consolidation.when_used: Multiple independent PRs need integrated testing

workflow.step_2_merge: Merge all feature branches into consolidation
workflow.step_3_test: Run full test suite on consolidated changes
workflow.step_4_verify: Verify no conflicts and all acceptance criteria met
workflow.step_5_review: Review consolidated changes for cross-PR issues
workflow.step_6_approve: Approve or request changes on individual PRs

parallel_execution.description: Support multi-agent parallel execution using git worktree
when_to_use: Execution plan indicates multiple agent delegation

copilot_cli_implementation_mode.description: Practical GitHub Copilot CLI with @orchestrator reading execution plans and delegating agents sequentially
advantages: Full context preservation across agent switches
No manual copy-paste of execution plans
Clean audit trail in single conversation
Execution plan becomes shared context for all agents

phase_1_orchestrator_invocation.user_prompt: @orchestrator read docs/implementation-planning/[PLAN].md and delegate appropriate agents per execution plan
phase_1_orchestrator_invocation.orchestrator_role: Read plan, analyze tasks, make intelligent delegation decisions

phase_2_delegation.orchestrator_decides: Based on plan analysis, which agent to invoke

delegation_triggers.to_developer: Issues requiring code implementation (features, bug fixes)

phase_3_sequential_execution.pattern: @developer works on issue #1 → issue #2 → ... per plan

phase_4_consolidation.task: Consolidate multiple PRs, verify integration per execution plan

phase_5_automation.task: Auto-merge approved PRs using gh CLI --merge

key_features: Implicit delegation (user doesn't specify agent, @orchestrator decides)
File reference triggers plan reading (user mentions @docs/path/to/plan.md)
Sequential agent execution in single terminal
Execution plan becomes shared, persistent context

orchestrator.read_plan_and_delegate: Read execution plan and delegate to appropriate agents
orchestrator.example: @orchestrator read docs/implementation-planning/[PLAN].md and delegate agents per plan
orchestrator.implicit_delegation: Orchestrator decides which agent based on plan analysis
orchestrator.feedback_cycle_monitoring: Monitor PR feedback cycles; check execution plan for Phase 3B-3F status
orchestrator.feature_branch_registry: Reference PR registry in execution plan to understand PR → branch mapping
orchestrator./plan: Create execution plan for multi-agent delegation

developer.implement_issue: Implement issue per execution plan (Phase 3A: Steps 1-10)
developer.example: @developer implement issue #35 per execution plan; git create/switch/implement/add/commit/push/PR
developer.feedback_workflow: Track feature branch from PR, switch to EXISTING branch, fix on same branch, push to same branch
developer.feedback_example: @developer handle feedback on PR #245: identify branch (gh pr view 245), switch to existing branch, implement fixes (steps 11-23), push to same branch
developer.critical_instruction: DO NOT create new branch (e.g., fix/pull-request-245-...). Reuse existing feature branch from PR.
developer.reference: See .copilot/PR_FEEDBACK_QUICK_REFERENCE.md for 9-step workflow or rules.json steps 11-23

reviewer.consolidate_prs: Consolidate multiple PRs and verify integration (Phase 4: after all feedback cycles complete)
reviewer.re_review_pr: Re-examine PR after developer pushes fixes (Phase 3E)
reviewer.example_consolidate: @reviewer consolidate Phase 4 PRs per execution plan; create work/phase-<N>-consolidation, merge, test, review, delete work branch
reviewer.example_examine: @reviewer examine PR #245: document findings, provide feedback comments
reviewer.example_re_review: @reviewer re-review PR #245 after developer fixes: check if all feedback addressed, approve or request more

tester.run_tests: Run test suite per execution plan
tester.example: @tester run integration tests on work/phase-4-consolidation branch

coordinator_agent.role: Synthesize specialist findings into single consolidated review
responsibilities: Deduplicate findings (same issue flagged by multiple agents)
coordinator_agent.output_format: Single GitHub comment with sections: Blockers | Major Issues | Minor Issues | Documentation
coordinator_agent.deduplication_algorithm: Group by file+line, keep highest severity, merge evidence

shared_state_object.error_log: Immutable record of failures and recoveries
shared_state_object.event_log: Immutable stream of all agent actions

synchronization.mechanism: Immutable event log applied to state
synchronization.benefit: Agents always see latest state, no stale assumptions
synchronization.audit_trail: Every change is logged and traceable

workflow.1_agents_read_spec: Before starting work, agent reads spec for context and constraints
workflow.2_agents_execute: Implement to spec; if need outside scope, create handoff request
workflow.3_spec_updates: Progress logged in spec; blockers documented immediately
workflow.4_feedback_loop: When spec unclear, agents request clarification via handoff
workflow.5_evolved_spec: Spec evolves with learnings; becomes self-documenting history

benefits: Single interpretation (all agents reading same spec, not repeating via prompt)

preconditions.validation: List all dependencies; mark as blocking or informational; only proceed if zero blocking
Create ownership matrix; verify no two agents touch same file; document handoffs
Each agent gets <1k tokens shared state, not 20k conversation
Different work types (code, tests, docs) can run in parallel safely

efficiency_targets.example: 3 tasks × 60 min = 180 min sequential → 50-60 min parallel (72% reduction)
efficiency_targets.scaling: Up to 5 agents sustainable; beyond requires careful governance

conflict_prevention.strategy_2_sequential_merging: Merge agent 1 → rebase others → merge agent 2 → rebase agent 3
conflict_prevention.strategy_3_ai_resolution: AI can resolve simple conflicts (list merges); human for complex

retry_strategy.escalation: After 3 failures, escalate to human with full context

memory_system.success_patterns: Track what worked (for future similar tasks)
memory_system.failure_patterns: Track what didn't work (avoid repeat mistakes)

error_recovery_config.self_validation: Agent validates output against explicit success criteria before returning

feedback_handling_best_practices.description: Best practices for handling PR review feedback efficiently and cleanly
feedback_handling_best_practices.core_principle: One issue = One feature branch = One PR = Clean merge

reuse_existing_branch.rule: REUSE existing feature branch from PR for feedback fixes
reuse_existing_branch.example_correct: git switch feat/issue-#35-add-skeleton-loading (identified via gh pr view)
reuse_existing_branch.example_wrong: git branch fix/pull-request-245-skeleton (NEVER create new branch)
reuse_existing_branch.reason: Prevents merge complexity of multiple branches per issue

no_new_pr_for_fixes.rule: DO NOT create new PR for feedback fixes
no_new_pr_for_fixes.mechanism: Push to existing branch; PR auto-updates
no_new_pr_for_fixes.benefit: Single PR per issue = clear history

stage_only_fix_files.rule: Only stage files modified for feedback fixes
stage_only_fix_files.wrong: git add . or git add -A (stages everything)

feedback_cycle_loop.description: Handle multiple feedback cycles until approved
cycle_structure: Phase 3B: Reviewer examines PR, documents findings
Phase 3C: Developer implements fixes on existing branch (steps 11-23)
Phase 3D: Developer pushes to same branch (PR auto-updates)
Phase 3E: Reviewer re-reviews; loop to 3C if more fixes needed, else approve
feedback_cycle_loop.loop_indicator: Tracking in execution plan shows which cycle (Cycle 1, 2, 3, ...)

execution_plan_tracking.pr_registry: Maintain table: PR # → Feature Branch → Issue → Cycle Status
execution_plan_tracking.feedback_log: For each PR, document: what feedback was received, what was fixed per cycle
execution_plan_tracking.progress_visibility: Clear audit trail of feedback addressed per cycle

when_to_consolidate.precondition: All PRs must complete feedback cycles (Phase 3F: Approved)
when_to_consolidate.testing: Merge all approved feature branches, run tests
when_to_consolidate.verification: Verify integration, then approve individual PRs for merge

metadata.purpose: GitHub Copilot CLI with PR feedback cycle handling and parallel multi-agent execution
metadata.target_audience: Development teams implementing parallel AI workflows with PR feedback cycles
metadata.enhancement_focus: PR Feedback Cycle (Phases 3B-3F) with existing feature branch reuse
related_files: rules.json - Agent approval, specialist roles, PR feedback steps (11-23)
docs/implementation-planning/ - Execution plans with feedback tracking
